# satgat

satgat is a Korean AI document generator. The Korean name is 삿갓. It turns natural-language briefs into structured Korean documents rendered with clean baekjaji/dak paper tones, ink typography, seals, restrained dancheong accents, and print/PDF-aware layout rules.

Repository: https://github.com/unclejobs-ai/satgat
Public origin: https://github.com/unclejobs-ai/satgat.git
License: MIT

Primary audience:
- Korean users writing documents for hiring, proposals, company profiles, newsletters, invitations, portfolios, product brochures, brand pages, and investor decks.

Core workflow:
1. User writes a Korean natural-language brief.
2. The generator selects a document type and fills structured JSON slots.
3. satgat templates render the output as HTML using Korean document design rules.
4. The result can be previewed, captured as PNG, or saved as PDF.

Important paths:
- README.md: project overview and visual examples.
- public/satgat/satgat-ko.html: static Korean landing/specimen page.
- public/satgat/assets/examples/ko/: Korean demo pack with HTML, PDF, PNG, and manifest files.
- scripts/generate-korean-demo-pack.mjs: regenerates Korean demo HTML and manifest.
- src/lib/templates/: runtime template definitions and registry.
- references/: Korean writing and design guidance.

Korean demo pack:
- resume-kim-sumin: Korean resume.
- self-intro-yoon-hajin: Korean self-introduction letter.
- proposal-hanji-retail: Korean business proposal.
- company-profile-dalbit: Korean company profile.
- investor-deck-maruai: Korean investor deck.
- invitation-jisoo-minho: Korean wedding invitation.

Design rules:
- Avoid pure white document backgrounds.
- Use clean baekjaji/dak-paper surfaces, not yellow parchment.
- Use ink-black body text, restrained dancheong red, jade, and gold accents.
- Prefer Korean serif hierarchy for document pages.
- Avoid generic SaaS document styling, fake traditional decoration, and translated-template tone.

Commands:
- npm run dev
- npm run demo:ko
- npm run build
- npm run lint
